_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 19:16:10Z)**_

Your SEO and OG setup is solid. Here's the audit:

**What's good:**
- `<title>`, `meta description`, `canonical`, `robots` all present and correct
- OG tags (`og:url`, `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:type`) complete
- Twitter Card tags with `summary_large_image` and matching title/description/image
- JSON-LD structured data (`WebApplication` schema) with author info
- `lang="en"` on `<html>`, `theme-color` set, favicon linked
- Semantic HTML (`<header>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<figure>`, `<nav>`, `<time>`)

**Issues / suggestions:**

1. **OG image is dynamic** — `og:image` points to `api.nasapicture.com/image-sd.jpg` which changes daily. Social crawlers cache aggressively (Facebook caches for 30 days, Twitter/X for ~7 days). This means the preview image will often be stale. Consider whether a static branded preview image would be better for consistent sharing, or if "whatever today's APOD was when it was crawled" is acceptable.

2. **Missing `og:image:width` / `og:image:height`** — Adding dimensions helps crawlers render previews without fetching the image first. Since the APOD varies, you could add approximate values (e.g., `1024x1024`) or skip this if the dynamic nature makes it impractical.

3. **Missing `og:site_name`** — Minor, but `<meta property="og:site_name" content="nasapicture.com">` gives platforms a display name separate from the title.

4. **Missing `og:locale`** — `<meta property="og:locale" content="en_US">` is optional but recommended.

5. **JSON-LD `offers` is minimal** — `"price": "0"` without `"priceCurrency"` will trigger warnings in Google's Rich Results validator. Add `"priceCurrency": "USD"` or remove the `offers` block entirely since it's a free tool, not a product listing.

6. **Empty `<nav>`** — The nav element at line 50-51 is empty. Screen readers will announce an empty navigation landmark. Either remove it or populate it.

None of these are critical blockers, but #1 (dynamic OG image caching) and #5 (JSON-LD warning) are the most worth addressing. Want me to fix any of these?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 21:07:44Z)**_

Updated both `og:image` and `twitter:image` to point to `/og-image.webp`. That solves the stale caching issue from #1 too, since it's now a static branded image instead of the daily-changing APOD.

One note: some platforms (Facebook, LinkedIn) have limited webp support for OG images. If you notice preview issues, a PNG or JPG fallback would be safer. Most modern crawlers handle webp fine though.

_**User (2026-04-06 21:45:43Z)**_

what's theme_color for in the manifest

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 21:45:48Z)**_



`theme_color` in the manifest tints the browser's UI chrome (address bar, status bar, task switcher) on mobile when the site is added to the home screen or opened as a PWA. It's essentially the same role as `<meta name="theme-color">` in your HTML, but for the installed app context.

Since yours is a dark site, `#0a0e17` keeps the browser chrome consistent with the page background instead of flashing white.

## Board System Structure

The project uses a hierarchical board system organized in `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/board/`. Here's the breakdown:

### Directory Structure

```
board/
├── .doc-ids                    # Document ID tracking
├── tasks/                      # Active tasks (in progress)
│   ├── TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md
│   └── TASK-0011_add-r2-storage-layer.md
├── planning/                   # Planned work (EPICs and NOTEs)
│   ├── EPIC-0002_landing-page-polish.md
│   ├── EPIC-0003_infrastructure-and-devops.md
│   └── NOTE-*_*.md (9 planning notes)
├── archive/                    # Completed work
│   ├── TASK-0001 through TASK-0017 (completed tasks)
│   ├── EPIC-0001 (completed epic)
│   └── NOTE-*_* (completed notes)
├── reference/
└── resources/
```

### File Naming Convention

Items follow the pattern: `{TYPE}-{ID}_{slug-title}.md`

- **TYPE**: `TASK`, `EPIC`, or `NOTE`
- **ID**: Zero-padded sequential number (0001, 0002, etc.)
- **Slug**: Kebab-case title (e.g., `nasa-data-insignia`, `add-r2-storage-layer`)

### TASK File Format

Every TASK file uses YAML frontmatter followed by Markdown:

```yaml
---
type: task
status: todo        # or: in-progress, done, blocked
created: 2026-03-26
parent: EPIC-0003   # (optional) parent epic
blocked-by: TASK-0010  # (optional) blocking task ID
---

# Task Title

Description and context...

## Subsections (optional)

- Detailed breakdown
- Checklist items
- Implementation notes
```

### Examples Provided

1. **TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md** — Simple task with basic metadata and bullet points
2. **TASK-0011_add-r2-storage-layer.md** — Complex task with sections for Infrastructure, Implementation, Verification, etc.

The system supports tracking task dependencies (`blocked-by`), parent epics (`parent`), status transitions, and creation dates. Active work lives in `tasks/`, completed items move to `archive/`, and future work planning stays in `planning/`.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-06 21:55:54Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/tasks/TASK-0011_add-r2-storage-layer.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: task
3	status: todo
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	parent: EPIC-0003
6	blocked-by: TASK-0010
7	---
8	
9	# Add R2 storage layer for APOD image archiving
10	
11	Add Cloudflare R2 as a persistent storage/origin layer between edge cache and NASA API. Every image fetched from NASA gets stored in R2, building an automatic archive and providing resilience if NASA is slow or down.
12	
13	## Infrastructure
14	
15	- Create R2 bucket (e.g., `apod-archive`)
16	- Add R2 binding in `wrangler.toml`: `[[r2_buckets]]` with `binding = "APOD_ARCHIVE"`
17	- Update `Env` interface in `src/index.ts` to include the R2 binding
18	
19	## R2 key structure
20	
21	- `{date}/hd.{ext}` — HD image
22	- `{date}/sd.{ext}` — SD image
23	- `{date}/info.json` — metadata snapshot
24	
25	## Worker changes (`src/index.ts`)
26	
27	- Refactor image fetching to layered lookup: Edge cache → R2 → NASA
28	- On R2 miss: fetch from NASA, store in R2 (`bucket.put()`), then serve
29	- On R2 hit: serve directly from R2 with appropriate `Content-Type`
30	- `/optimized` applies `cf.image` transforms on top of R2-served images
31	- Cache metadata in R2 alongside images
32	
33	## Edge caching preserved
34	
35	- `cf.cacheTtl` and `cf.cacheEverything` remain on all responses
36	- R2 replaces NASA as the origin; edge cache sits in front
37	
38	## Cache invalidation
39	
40	Once an image is in R2, it's permanent — if NASA corrects or replaces an image at the same URL, we'd serve the stale version. Low risk (NASA rarely edits published APODs) but worth a safety valve:
41	
42	- For the current day's image, treat R2 as a cache with a short TTL (e.g., re-fetch from NASA if R2 object is <1 hour old and edge cache misses) — allows corrections to propagate same-day
43	- For past dates, serve from R2 unconditionally (immutable after rollover)
44	- Consider an auth-protected manual purge endpoint (e.g., `DELETE /admin/purge/{date}`) to force re-fetch from NASA on demand
45	
46	## Verification
47	
48	- First request stores to R2 (check via `wrangler r2 object get`)
49	- Subsequent requests serve from R2 without hitting NASA
50	- Edge caching still works in front of R2
51	- `/optimized` transforms apply correctly on R2-sourced images
52	
[result-id: r5]
```
</details></tool-use>

### File Naming Convention

Items follow the pattern: `{TYPE}-{ID}_{slug-title}.md`

- **TYPE**: `TASK`, `EPIC`, or `NOTE`
- **ID**: Zero-padded sequential number (0001, 0002, etc.)
- **Slug**: Kebab-case title (e.g., `nasa-data-insignia`, `add-r2-storage-layer`)

### TASK File Format

<command-message>board:add</command-message>
<command-name>/board:add</command-name>
<command-args>NOTE: api.nasapicture.com on twitter/x.com doesn't show an image, bc card previews require HTML with OG image. more info: ### Problem
Twitter/Facebook/etc. link previews need an HTML page with Open Graph meta tags. When their crawlers hit `api.nasapicture.com/`, the Worker responds with `Content-Type: image/jpeg` (the raw APOD image), so there's no HTML to parse and no card renders. This means tweets linking `api.nasapicture.com` show no preview image.

### Fix
In the Worker, sniff the `User-Agent` header on incoming requests to `api.nasapicture.com` routes (at minimum `/`, maybe `/hd` and `/sd` too). If the UA matches a known social crawler, return an HTML document with OG tags instead of the image bytes. Otherwise, behave as today and return the image.

### Crawler User-Agents to match (case-insensitive substring)
- `Twitterbot`
- `facebookexternalhit`
- `Facebot`
- `Slackbot`
- `Discordbot`
- `LinkedInBot`
- `TelegramBot`
- `WhatsApp`
- `Pinterest`
- `redditbot`

### HTML response shape
Minimal document with:
- `<title>` and `<meta name="description">`
- `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:type=website`, `og:url`
- `og:image` → absolute URL to the image (e.g. `https://api.nasapicture.com/` or `/sd` for a smaller card-friendly version)
- `og:image:width` / `og:image:height` if known from APOD metadata
- `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:image`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`</command-args>

Base directory for this skill: /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/skills/add

# Add Board Item

Create a new item in the `board/` project management system. The system spec is bundled with the plugin at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`.

## Existing Board Items

Pre-loaded board data for duplicate detection:

filepath	type	status	priority	blocked_by	parent	title
./board/tasks/TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Apply for NASA Data Insignia
./board/tasks/TASK-0011_add-r2-storage-layer.md	task	todo	-	-	EPIC-0003	Add R2 storage layer for APOD image archiving
./board/planning/EPIC-0002_landing-page-polish.md	epic	planning	-	-	-	Landing Page Polish
./board/planning/EPIC-0003_infrastructure-and-devops.md	epic	planning	-	-	-	Infrastructure and DevOps
./board/planning/NOTE-0005_add-individual-metadata-routes.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add individual metadata routes
./board/planning/NOTE-0006_add-why-is-this-needed-section.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add a "Why Is This Needed" Section That Explains the APOD API
./board/planning/NOTE-0008_generate-daily-og-image.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Generate a daily OG image
./board/planning/NOTE-0010_auto-refresh-landing-page-on-rollover.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Auto-refresh landing page on APOD rollover
./board/planning/NOTE-0014_add-gh-action-auto-deploy-on-merge.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add GitHub Action to auto-deploy to Cloudflare on PR merge into main
./board/planning/NOTE-0016_local-timezone-apod-rollover.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Local timezone APOD rollover
./board/planning/NOTE-0017_landing-page-critique-apr-2026.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page design critique (April 2026)

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.

---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-06

# Serve OG HTML to social crawlers on api.nasapicture.com

## Problem

Twitter/Facebook/etc. link previews need an HTML page with Open Graph meta tags. When their crawlers hit `api.nasapicture.com/`, the Worker responds with `Content-Type: image/jpeg` (the raw APOD image), so there's no HTML to parse and no card renders. This means tweets linking `api.nasapicture.com` show no preview image.

## Fix

In the Worker, sniff the `User-Agent` header on incoming requests to `api.nasapicture.com` routes (at minimum `/`, maybe `/hd` and `/sd` too). If the UA matches a known social crawler, return an HTML document with OG tags instead of the image bytes. Otherwise, behave as today and return the image.

## Crawler User-Agents to match (case-insensitive substring)

- `Twitterbot`
- `facebookexternalhit`
- `Facebot`
- `Slackbot`
- `Discordbot`
- `LinkedInBot`
- `TelegramBot`
- `WhatsApp`
- `Pinterest`
- `redditbot`

## HTML response shape

Minimal document with:

- `<title>` and `<meta name="description">`
- `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:type=website`, `og:url`
- `og:image` — absolute URL to the image (e.g. `https://api.nasapicture.com/` or `/sd` for a smaller card-friendly version)
- `og:image:width` / `og:image:height` if known from APOD metadata
- `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:image`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`

```

File created successfully at: /Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/board/planning/NOTE-0020_serve-og-html-to-social-crawlers.md

325	          fit: "scale-down",
326	          width: 1200,
327	          quality: 85,
328	        };
329	
330	        const accept = request.headers.get("Accept") || "";
331	        if (/image\/avif/.test(accept)) {
332	          imageOpts.format = "avif";
333	        } else if (/image\/webp/.test(accept)) {
334	          imageOpts.format = "webp";
335	        }
336	
337	        log.push(`  [optimized] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → cf.image ${JSON.stringify(imageOpts)}`);
338	
339	        const upstream = await fetch(imageUrl, {
340	          cf: { image: imageOpts },
341	        });
342	
343	        if (!upstream.ok) {
344	          throw new Error(`Image transform failed: ${upstream.status}`);
345	        }
346	
347	        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
348	
349	        res = new Response(upstream.body, {
350	          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
351	        });
352	      } else if (thumbPaths.includes(pathname)) {
353	        const apod = await findLatestImage(log, apiKey);
354	        const imageUrl = apod.hdurl || apod.url;
355	        validateImageUrl(imageUrl);
356	
357	        const imageOpts: RequestInitCfPropertiesImage = {
358	          fit: "scale-down",
359	          width: 32,
360	          quality: 30,
361	          format: "webp",
362	          blur: 5,
363	        };
364	
365	        log.push(`  [thumb] ${imageUrl.slice(0, 80)}… → cf.image ${JSON.stringify(imageOpts)}`);
366	
367	        const upstream = await fetch(imageUrl, {
368	          cf: { image: imageOpts },
369	        });
370	
371	        if (!upstream.ok) {
372	          throw new Error(`Thumbnail transform failed: ${upstream.status}`);
373	        }
374	
375	        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/webp";
376	
377	        res = new Response(upstream.body, {
378	          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
379	        });
380	      } else if (pathname === "/info") {
381	        const endpoints = {
382	          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
383	          "/image.jpg": "Same as / with file extension (for contexts that need one)",
384	          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
385	          "/image-sd.jpg": "Same as /sd with file extension",
386	          "/optimized": "Optimized image (≤1200px, auto WebP/AVIF)",
387	          "/image-optimized.jpg": "Same as /optimized with file extension",
388	          "/thumb": "Tiny blurred thumbnail (32px WebP, ~1KB)",
389	          "/thumb.jpg": "Same as /thumb with file extension",
390	          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD (includes image dimensions)",
391	          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
392	        };
393	
394	        try {
395	          const apod = await fetchApod(log, apiKey);
396	          const imageUrl = apod.media_type === "image" ? (apod.hdurl || apod.url) : null;
397	          const dimensions = imageUrl ? await fetchImageDimensions(log, imageUrl) : null;
398	
399	          res = new Response(
400	            JSON.stringify({
401	              title: apod.title,
402	              date: apod.date,
403	              explanation: apod.explanation,
404	              media_type: apod.media_type,
405	              url: apod.url,

413	          );
414	        } catch (err) {
415	          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
416	          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;

```md
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-06
---

# Serve OG HTML to social crawlers on api.nasapicture.com

## Problem

Twitter/Facebook/etc. link previews need an HTML page with Open Graph meta tags. When their crawlers hit `api.nasapicture.com/`, the Worker responds with `Content-Type: image/jpeg` (the raw APOD image), so there's no HTML to parse and no card renders. This means tweets linking `api.nasapicture.com` show no preview image.

## Fix

In the Worker, sniff the `User-Agent` header on incoming requests to the image routes. If the UA matches a known social crawler, return an HTML document with OG tags instead of the image bytes. Otherwise, behave as today and return the image.

## Affected routes

The crawler check should run before the image response for these route groups in `src/index.ts`:

- `hdPaths`: `/`, `/image.jpg`
- `sdPaths`: `/sd`, `/image-sd.jpg`
- `optimizedPaths`: `/optimized`, `/image-optimized.jpg`
- `thumbPaths`: `/thumb`, `/thumb.jpg`

The `/info` and `/about` routes already return text, so they don't need this.

## Implementation approach

1. **Add a `isSocialCrawler(request)` helper** that reads `User-Agent` and tests against a regex of known crawler substrings (case-insensitive).

2. **Add a `buildOgHtml(apod, requestUrl)` helper** that takes the APOD metadata (already fetched by `findLatestImage`) and the request URL, and returns a minimal HTML string with OG tags.

3. **Insert the check early in each image route block**, after `findLatestImage` resolves but before fetching the upstream image. If it's a crawler, return the OG HTML and skip the image fetch entirely (saves bandwidth and latency).

\```
if (isSocialCrawler(request)) {
  const apod = await findLatestImage(log, apiKey);
  res = new Response(buildOgHtml(apod, reqUrl), {
    headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" }),
  });
}
\```

Since `findLatestImage` is already called for these routes, the crawler path just skips the image fetch/transform step.

## Crawler User-Agents to match (case-insensitive substring)

- `Twitterbot`
- `facebookexternalhit`
- `Facebot`
- `Slackbot`
- `Discordbot`
- `LinkedInBot`
- `TelegramBot`
- `WhatsApp`
- `Pinterest`
- `redditbot`

## HTML response shape

Minimal document with:

- `<title>` — APOD title + "| nasapicture.com"
- `<meta name="description">` — truncated APOD explanation
- `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:type=website`, `og:url`
- `og:image` — `https://api.nasapicture.com/sd` (standard res is more reliable for card dimensions)
- `og:image:width` / `og:image:height` — from `fetchImageDimensions` if available, omit if not
- `og:site_name` — "nasapicture.com"
- `twitter:card=summary_large_image`, `twitter:image`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`

## Caching consideration

Crawler responses can use the same cache TTL as regular image responses. The OG HTML is date-keyed (same APOD metadata), so it rolls over at midnight ET like everything else. Consider using a distinct cache key suffix (e.g., `:og`) to avoid serving HTML from the image cache or vice versa.

## Testing

- Use `curl -A "Twitterbot/1.0"` to verify the crawler path returns HTML
- Use `curl` with no special UA to verify normal image behavior is unchanged
- Validate the returned HTML with the [Twitter Card Validator](https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator) and [Facebook Sharing Debugger](https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/)

```

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.

---

# Add a user-facing gallery of past APOD images

A browsable archive/gallery on the landing page (or a dedicated route) showing past Astronomy Pictures of the Day. Depends on TASK-0011 (R2 storage layer) being in place to persist historical images.

## Why

The daily APOD is ephemeral by design, but visitors naturally want to browse past images. A gallery turns the site from a single-serving utility into something worth revisiting. NASA's own APOD archive is functional but not visually engaging, so there's room to offer a better browsing experience.

## Open questions

- **Scope**: gallery on the landing page itself, or a separate `/gallery` route?
- **Data source**: R2 for images, but where does the metadata (title, date, explanation) come from? Could store alongside images in R2, or fetch from NASA's APOD API by date on demand.
- **Depth**: how far back? All of R2 history (from when archiving starts), or backfill older images?
- **Navigation**: calendar picker, infinite scroll, grid with lightbox?
- **Performance**: lazy loading, thumbnail grid using the `/thumb` transform, full image on click

```

```text
1	---
2	type: task
3	status: todo
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	parent: EPIC-0003
6	blocked-by: TASK-0010
7	---
8	
9	# Add R2 storage layer for APOD image archiving
10	
11	Add Cloudflare R2 as a persistent storage/origin layer between edge cache and NASA API. Every image fetched from NASA gets stored in R2, building an automatic archive and providing resilience if NASA is slow or down.
12	
13	## Infrastructure
14	
15	- Create R2 bucket (e.g., `apod-archive`)
16	- Add R2 binding in `wrangler.toml`: `[[r2_buckets]]` with `binding = "APOD_ARCHIVE"`
17	- Update `Env` interface in `src/index.ts` to include the R2 binding
18	
19	## R2 key structure
20	
21	- `{date}/hd.{ext}` — HD image
22	- `{date}/sd.{ext}` — SD image
23	- `{date}/info.json` — metadata snapshot
24	
25	## Worker changes (`src/index.ts`)
26	
27	- Refactor image fetching to layered lookup: Edge cache → R2 → NASA
28	- On R2 miss: fetch from NASA, store in R2 (`bucket.put()`), then serve
29	- On R2 hit: serve directly from R2 with appropriate `Content-Type`
30	- `/optimized` applies `cf.image` transforms on top of R2-served images
31	- Cache metadata in R2 alongside images
32	
33	## Edge caching preserved
34	
35	- `cf.cacheTtl` and `cf.cacheEverything` remain on all responses
36	- R2 replaces NASA as the origin; edge cache sits in front
37	
38	## Cache invalidation
39	
40	Once an image is in R2, it's permanent — if NASA corrects or replaces an image at the same URL, we'd serve the stale version. Low risk (NASA rarely edits published APODs) but worth a safety valve:
41	
42	- For the current day's image, treat R2 as a cache with a short TTL (e.g., re-fetch from NASA if R2 object is <1 hour old and edge cache misses) — allows corrections to propagate same-day
43	- For past dates, serve from R2 unconditionally (immutable after rollover)
44	- Consider an auth-protected manual purge endpoint (e.g., `DELETE /admin/purge/{date}`) to force re-fetch from NASA on demand
45	
46	## Verification
47	
48	- First request stores to R2 (check via `wrangler r2 object get`)
49	- Subsequent requests serve from R2 without hitting NASA
50	- Edge caching still works in front of R2
51	- `/optimized` transforms apply correctly on R2-sourced images
52	
[result-id: r15]
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-30
5	---
6	
7	# Add individual metadata routes
8	
9	Add dedicated routes for individual APOD metadata fields, so consumers can fetch just what they need without parsing JSON from `/info`:
10	
11	- `/date` — today's APOD date (e.g. `2026-04-05`)
12	- `/title` — image title
13	- `/explanation` — description text
14	- `/copyright` — copyright holder, or `none` if public domain
15	
16	Returns plain text (`text/plain; charset=utf-8`). Useful for shell scripts, widgets, and other lightweight integrations.
17	
18	## Implementation details
19	
20	### Routing
21	
22	All four routes live in `src/index.ts` alongside the existing route branches. Add a single block after the `/about` handler that matches any of the four paths, calls `fetchApod()` once, then returns the requested field. No `.jpg` aliases needed since these are text endpoints.
23	
24	```
25	} else if (["/date", "/title", "/explanation", "/copyright"].includes(pathname)) {
26	  const apod = await fetchApod(log, apiKey);
27	  const fieldMap: Record<string, string> = {
28	    "/date": apod.date,
29	    "/title": apod.title,
30	    "/explanation": apod.explanation,
31	    "/copyright": apod.copyright ?? "none",
32	  };
33	  res = new Response(fieldMap[pathname], {
34	    headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
35	  });
36	}
37	```
38	
39	### Caching
40	
41	No new cache logic required. These routes call `fetchApod()`, which already caches the NASA API response with the standard 6h primary / 24h stale strategy. The `baseHeaders()` helper sets `Cache-Control: public, max-age=21600` on the response, so Cloudflare's edge and browsers will cache the plain-text responses too.
42	
43	### Response behavior
44	
45	- Uses `fetchApod()` (today's date), not `findLatestImage()`, since metadata should reflect whatever today's APOD actually is, even if it's a video.
46	- `/copyright` returns the literal string `none` when the field is absent (public domain images). An empty 200 response would be ambiguous for shell consumers piping output.
47	- Error handling falls through to the existing outer `catch` block, which returns JSON error responses with appropriate status codes (429 for rate limits, 502 otherwise).
48	
49	### Discoverability
50	
51	After adding the routes:
52	
53	1. **`/info` endpoint map** — add all four paths to the `endpoints` object so they appear in the JSON response.
54	2. **`/about` ASCII art** — add the routes to the plain-text route listing.
55	3. **`baseHeaders` Link header** — optionally add `rel="describedby"` links for `/title` and `/date`, though this is low priority since the Link header is already dense.
56	4. **404 message** — update the fallback error string to mention the new routes.
57	5. **Landing page** — if the landing page lists available endpoints, update it there too.
58	
59	### Testing
60	
61	Verify manually with `curl`:
62	
63	```bash
64	curl -s https://api.nasapicture.com/date
65	curl -s https://api.nasapicture.com/title
66	curl -s https://api.nasapicture.com/explanation
67	curl -s https://api.nasapicture.com/copyright
68	```
69	
70	Confirm each returns plain text with correct `Content-Type` and `Cache-Control` headers. Test a video APOD day to verify `/copyright` returns `none` gracefully.
71

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0008_generate-daily-og-image.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Generate a daily OG image
8	
9	Generate a new Open Graph image every day, resized and cropped to the optimal 1200x630px dimensions. Adding a text overlay with the logo would be a bonus.
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	The current OG image points to `https://api.nasapicture.com/image.jpg`, which serves the raw APOD at whatever dimensions NASA provides (often square or portrait). Social platforms expect 1200x630px for optimal previews.
14	
15	## Recommended approach: `workers-og`
16	
17	[`workers-og`](https://github.com/kvnang/workers-og) renders HTML/CSS to PNG entirely inside a Worker (Satori → SVG → PNG, no browser needed). This is purpose-built for OG image generation at the edge.
18	
19	**How it would work:**
20	1. Add a `/og` route to the existing Worker (or a separate one)
21	2. Fetch today's APOD metadata from `/info` (title, date)
22	3. Render an HTML template with the APOD photo as background, title text, and nasapicture.com branding
23	4. Cache the result aggressively (24h TTL keyed to date) — one cold generation per day
24	5. Update the `og:image` meta tag to point to the new `/og` route
25	
26	**Pros:** Single Worker, zero external dependencies, familiar HTML/CSS for layout, no billing (stays within free Workers tier since we're generating the image ourselves, not using Cloudflare's Images API).
27	
28	**Cons:** Satori supports flexbox only (no CSS grid). Custom fonts need to be embedded as ArrayBuffers (adds to bundle size). Subset of CSS supported.
29	
30	## Alternative: Cloudflare Images binding
31	
32	Use the Images binding (`env.IMAGES`) to resize/crop the APOD to 1200x630 natively, then `.draw()` a pre-rendered text overlay PNG on top.
33	
34	- Requires `[images] binding = "IMAGES"` in `wrangler.toml`
35	- Free tier: 5,000 unique transformations/month (we'd use ~30/month — well within limits)
36	- Higher-fidelity resizing with smart crop/gravity options
37	- More moving parts (two-step: resize + composite)
38	
39	This is the upgrade path if `workers-og` quality isn't sufficient or if we need smarter cropping (e.g. face/subject detection).
40	
41	## Not viable
42	
43	- **Sharp** — uses native Node.js modules, incompatible with Workers
44	- **Canvas APIs** — no browser context in Workers
45	
46	## Useful links
47	
48	- [workers-og](https://github.com/kvnang/workers-og)
49	- [@cf-wasm/photon](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cf-wasm/photon) — WASM image processing if more control is needed
50	- [Cloudflare Images: draw overlays](https://developers.cloudflare.com/images/transform-images/draw-overlays/)
51	- [Cloudflare Images pricing](https://developers.cloudflare.com/images/pricing/) — free up to 5,000 transformations/month
52	
[result-id: r18]
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./board/planning/NOTE-0016_local-timezone-apod-rollover.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	---
6	
7	# Local timezone APOD rollover
8	
9	Users might find it useful (both in the API and the landing page) to choose to get the new image at midnight in their local timezone, instead of when it actually rolls over in ET.
10	
11	## The problem
12	
13	APOD publishes once per day, keyed to Eastern Time. A user in Tokyo (UTC+9) sees the image roll over at 1pm JST -- awkward if they want "a new picture every morning." Conversely, a user in PST sees it change at 9pm, which might actually be fine.
14	
15	## Possible API surface
16	
17	A query parameter like `?tz=Asia/Tokyo` or `?tz=UTC%2B9` on any image endpoint. The Worker would compute "today" in the requested timezone instead of ET, and request that date's APOD.
18	
19	## Key constraint
20	
21	If a user's local date is ahead of ET (e.g., it's April 2 in Tokyo but still April 1 in ET), the April 2 APOD doesn't exist yet. The Worker would need to fall back to the most recent available date, which is what `findLatestImage` already does via lookback. So this might mostly work out of the box -- the timezone param just changes which date the lookback starts from.
22	
23	## Landing page
24	
25	Could detect timezone via `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone` and pass it to the API. Or let users toggle between "NASA time" and "local time" if we want to keep it explicit.
26	
27	## Phasing
28	
29	Nice-to-have, not critical. Phase 1 could just handle timezones behind ET (west of Eastern), which is straightforward -- the requested date always exists. Timezones ahead of ET (where the date might not exist yet) can come later if there's demand.
30	
31	## Related
32	
33	Ties naturally into TASK-0011 (R2 storage layer for image archiving). If we're storing the last N images anyway, serving a timezone-shifted "yesterday" from the archive is trivial. Any tasks spawned from this note are likely blocked by TASK-0011.
34	
35	## Open questions
36	
37	- Caching implications: timezone-varied requests would reduce cache hit rates since each timezone produces a different date key during the rollover window
38	
[result-id: r19]
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: 2026-04-06
blocked-by: TASK-0011
---

# Add a user-facing gallery of past APOD images

A browsable archive/gallery showing past Astronomy Pictures of the Day. Depends on TASK-0011 (R2 storage layer) being in place to persist historical images.

## Why

The daily APOD is ephemeral by design, but visitors naturally want to browse past images. A gallery turns the site from a single-serving utility into something worth revisiting. NASA's own APOD archive is functional but not visually engaging, so there's room to offer a better browsing experience that matches the project's aesthetic.

## Data available from R2

TASK-0011 defines the R2 key structure as:

- `{date}/hd.{ext}` — HD image
- `{date}/sd.{ext}` — SD image
- `{date}/info.json` — metadata snapshot (title, explanation, copyright, dimensions)

The `info.json` sidecar gives us everything we need for gallery cards without hitting NASA's API. The archive builds organically from the day R2 goes live, one image per day.

## API surface

The gallery needs a way to list and fetch archived images. Options:

- **New route: `/archive`** — returns JSON listing of available dates (from R2 `list()`)
- **New route: `/archive/{date}`** — returns image for a specific date (from R2)
- **New route: `/archive/{date}/info`** — returns metadata for a specific date

Alternatively, the existing `/info?date=2026-04-01` could accept a date param. But dedicated archive routes are cleaner and avoid overloading the "today" endpoints.

## Frontend

### Location
Could live on the landing page as a section below the hero, or as a dedicated `/gallery` page. A separate page avoids bloating the clean single-page landing, and gives room for richer browsing UX.

### Layout ideas
- Thumbnail grid using Cloudflare Image Transformations (same approach as `/thumb` route, but per-date)
- Click/tap to expand with title, explanation, and full-res image
- Date-based navigation (calendar picker or month groupings)
- Infinite scroll or paginated grid

### Performance
- Grid thumbnails via `cf.image` transforms (small, blurred placeholders with lazy-loaded sharper versions)
- Pre-sized containers using dimensions from `info.json` (same pattern as the current hero image)
- Paginate R2 `list()` calls to avoid loading the entire archive at once

## Related board items

- **TASK-0011** (blocker): R2 storage layer that archives images and metadata daily
- **EPIC-0002**: Landing page polish, gallery would be a major addition under this umbrella
- **NOTE-0005**: Individual metadata routes (`/title`, `/date`, etc.) could extend to per-date variants
- **NOTE-0008**: Daily OG image generation, could reuse the gallery's per-date image serving
- **NOTE-0016**: Local timezone rollover also depends on R2 and date-keyed image access

## Open questions

- Gallery on the landing page or a separate `/gallery` route?
- How far back to go? Only from when R2 archiving starts, or backfill older images from NASA's API?
- Search/filter by title or keyword, or just date-based browsing?
- Should the gallery be part of the Cloudflare Pages landing site, or served by the Worker?

```
